Defines data types and constants for handling ECMAScript strings and Unicode encoding in JerryScript.

# Purpose
This C header file, `lit_globals.h`, defines types and constants related to the representation and manipulation of strings and characters in the JerryScript engine, which is a lightweight JavaScript engine. It includes type definitions for `ecma_char_t`, `ecma_length_t`, and `lit_utf8_byte_t`, which represent 16-bit code units, string lengths, and UTF-8 bytes, respectively. The file also defines constants for the maximum number of bytes needed to encode code units and code points in UTF-8 and CESU-8 formats, such as `LIT_UTF8_MAX_BYTES_IN_CODE_UNIT` and `LIT_UTF8_MAX_BYTES_IN_CODE_POINT`. Additionally, it provides a hash constant, `LIT_STRING_LENGTH_HASH`, for the frequently used string "length" and a limit constant, `LIT_STRING_HASH_LIMIT`, to optimize performance on ARM architectures. The file includes detailed comments explaining the relationship between ECMAScript standards, UTF-16, and UTF-8 encoding, as well as the internal string representation used by JerryScript.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `jrt.h`
